symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid 3-day decline in fatigue and cold intolerance in treated autoimmune thyroid disease

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with a symptomatic flare in known autoimmune thyroid disease.
Severe hypothyroid-type symptoms are non-specific — do not by themselves indicate decompensation without objective danger signs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved alertness — fully conscious and conversational (no obtundation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced consciousness is the defining feature of myxoedema coma; a fully alert, conversational patient does not have it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal work of breathing — no dyspnoea or hypoventilation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoventilation is a feature of severe decompensation; its absence argues against the emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: No vomiting, abdominal pain, postural dizziness or hyperpigmentation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of vomiting, postural symptoms and pigmentation makes an Addisonian crisis unlikely on history.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent thyroid function test reportedly normal (patient-reported)

investigation_details.expected_result: Reportedly normal TSH (patient-reported, not independently verified) — argues against overt hypothyroid decompensation but does not replace fresh testing given the acute change.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without fresh repeat TFTs a genuine biochemical change since the last test could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recently normal TSH makes severe biochemical decompensation less likely, though repeat testing is still required.
A euthyroid biochemical picture is consistent with symptoms that are not driven by absolute hormone deficiency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: HR 60, BP 105/70, temp 36.4C, RR 14, SpO2 98% — haemodynamically stable, not hypothermic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stable haemodynamics and a low-normal temperature exclude decompensation — but these are sealed clinician measurements the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.
Normotension argues strongly against an adrenal crisis; sealed clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm mildly enlarged non-tender thyroid; dry cool skin; delayed-relaxation deep tendon reflexes on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Goitre and myxoedematous examination signs support the hypothyroid/autoimmune picture — sealed clinician findings not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor oral intake for ~24 hours; lives alone with no one checking in

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced intake is a marker of severity and social risk rather than a diagnostic discriminator; it raises urgency of support and review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Subjective severe cold intolerance; wearing multiple layers in a heated home

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cold intolerance is characteristic of hypothyroid symptomatology and supports a thyroid-related flare.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rapid fatigue + cold intolerance + myxoedematous examination signs together point to a symptomatic hypothyroid-type flare of the known autoimmune thyroid disease — but the pattern is benign in the absence of the objective danger signs (reduced consciousness, hypoventilation, haemodynamic instability).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, free T3)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Repeat TFTs are the key test to reassess thyroid status after a rapid symptomatic change, even with a reportedly recent normal TSH.
Confirms/monitors the autoimmune process; does not change acute management and should not be over-interpreted as the cause of symptoms.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Morning cortisol, electrolytes (sodium), FBC and CRP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for adrenal insufficiency, hyponatraemia and intercurrent infection as alternative causes of acute severe fatigue.
No focal neurological signs or features to justify neuroimaging — over-investigation for this presentation.
